# Notch So Good

> A pixel-art crab called Chawd lives in your MacBook's notch and watches your AI coding agents — Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI — so you don't have to.

## What it is

Notch So Good is a free, open-source macOS menubar app for developers who run AI coding agents. It places a tiny animated pixel-art crab mascot (Chawd) in your MacBook's notch. While an agent is working, a black pill extends the notch with Chawd and a live session timer. When the agent needs you — a question, a permission, a finished task — the notch expands into a color-coded notification. Click to jump back to the exact terminal tab.

## Key features

- Session pill: Dynamic Island-style pill with fluid concave notch curves, Chawd mascot + live timer
- Tool-aware mascot: Chawd wears glasses while the agent reads, holds a pencil while it edits, celebrates with confetti when a task completes
- Permission approvals from the notch: Allow / Always Allow / Deny buttons, plus global hotkeys ⌃⌥A / ⌃⌥D
- Usage limits: session (5-hour) and weekly rate-limit bars for both Claude and Codex, with reset countdowns and one warning before you hit the wall
- Real completion summaries: Stop notifications show the agent's actual last message from the transcript
- Shift report: today's sessions, completed tasks, and active time in the menu bar
- Notification history: recent alerts in the menu bar, click to jump back
- Nudge: one gentle re-ping if a session waits 2+ minutes — repeat hook events are deduped so it never spams
- Multi-agent, multi-session: Claude / Codex / Gemini badges, sessions grouped by project, subagent tree
- 17 idle animations, drowsiness system, menu bar attention dot
- Launch at Login, auto-update via Sparkle

## Install

brew install --cask deepshal99/tap/notch-so-good

Or: npx notch-so-good

Or: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/deepshal99/notch-so-good/main/get.sh | bash

## Requirements

- macOS 14+ (Sonoma or later)
- Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, or Gemini CLI
- MacBook with a notch (recommended — works without one too)

## How it works

Claude Code / Codex hooks send events over a local Unix domain socket (/tmp/notchsogood.sock) that the app listens on. Permission checks are bidirectional: the hook waits for your Allow/Deny from the notch. Usage limits are read from your own local logins — Claude Code's OAuth credentials (Keychain) against Anthropic's usage endpoint, and Codex CLI's ~/.codex/auth.json against the ChatGPT usage endpoint. No server, no accounts; optional anonymous stats are off unless the build ships a telemetry key.

- GitHub: https://github.com/deepshal99/notch-so-good
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/notch-so-good
